revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Awawa Jinja]][[ja:阿波々神社]] {{Expand Japanese|阿波々神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Awawa Shrine | native_name = 阿波々神社 | image = Awagatake03.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Awawa Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.841475|138.06764}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Awawa Shrine'''|阿波々神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tōtōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11642130|ltq=Q11642130|qid=Q11642130|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 52 ** P155: {{ill|Kotonomama Hachiman-gū|lt=Kotonomama Hachimangū|de|Kotonomama Hachiman-gū|en|Kotonomama Hachiman-gū|ja|事任八幡宮|zh|事任八幡宮|qid=Q11371148|1=Kotonomama Hachiman-gū}} ** P156: {{ill|Q118105171|ltq=Q118105171|qid=Q118105171|1=Toshi Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q823988|ltq=Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} * {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|lt=Tōtōmi Province|ckb|پارێزگای تۆتۆمی|cs|Tótómi (provincie)|de|Provinz Tōtōmi|en|Tōtōmi Province|eo|Provinco Tōtōmi|es|Provincia de Tōtōmi|fa|ولایت توتومی|fr|Province de Tōtōmi|gl|Provincia de Tōtōmi|id|Provinsi Tōtōmi|it|Tōtōmi|ja|遠江国|ko|도토미국|lt|Tōtōmio žemė|nl|Tōtōmi (provincie)|pt|Província de Tōtōmi|ru|Тотоми (провинция)|simple|Tōtōmi Province|sk|Tótómi|sr|Провинција Тотоми|tr|Tōtōmi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Тотомі|zh_min_nan|Tôtômi-kok|zh_yue|遠江國|zh|遠江國|qid=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}} * {{ill|Q11385111|ltq=Q11385111|qid=Q11385111|1=Saya district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.841475|138.06764}} == image (P18) == * Awagatake03.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181538 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あわわじんじゃhttps://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == postal code (P281) == * 436-0081https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3080405004429 == Commons category (P373) == * Awawa Jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県掛川市初馬5419', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Awawa Jinja}} [[JA:阿波々神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11657490}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Awawa Shrine |Image = [[Image:Awagatake03.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakegawa City|Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} Hatsuma 5419 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Awahime-no-Mikoto |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
{{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=736|Q23581|qid=Q23581|1=736}} |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|qid=Q2502|1=April 10}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Awawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Awawa Shrine''' (あわわじんじゃ, ''Awawa Jinja'') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hatsuma, {{ill|lt=Kakegawa City|Q823988|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is enshrined near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Awagatake (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q11604550|qid=Q11604550|lt=Mount Awagatake|1=Awa-ga-take}}. === Overview === [[Image:Awagatake04.jpg|thumb|200px|View of the surrounding area from the summit of [[Mount Awagatake (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Mount Awagatake]]]] The shrine enshrines Awahime-no-Mikoto as its {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} and was founded in {{ill|lt=736|Q23581|qid=Q23581|1=736}}“[https://web.archive.org/web/20090615071355/http://www10.plala.or.jp/awawajinjya/sub1.html#Anchor-14490 History]” Awawa Shrine.. Within the {{ill|lt=precincts|Q11429002|qid=Q11429002|1=keidai}} of Awawa Shrine, there is also {{ill|lt=Yaegaki Shrine|Q3571285|qid=Q3571285|1=Yaegaki Shrine}}, which enshrines {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinada-hime|1=Kushinadahime}}. The surrounding area is covered with {{ill|lt=evergreen broadleaf forest|Q788099|qid=Q788099|1=Laurel forest}}. This forest has been designated by Kakegawa City as a {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|qid=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} under the name “Shrine Grove of Awawa Shrine” and is protected as a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}}“[https://web.archive.org/web/20090615065637/http://www10.plala.or.jp/awawajinjya/sub9saiji.html Seasonal Festival and Prayer Information at Awawa Shrine]” Awawa Shrine.Kakegawa City Board of Education, Lifelong Learning Division, Cultural Promotion Office, Cultural Properties Section, “[https://web.archive.org/web/20071208174729/http://lgportal.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/kanko/bunkazai/sisiteibunkazai/sitennenkinenbutu.jsp City-designated Natural Monuments]”, Kakegawa City Hall, {{ill|lt=April 1, 2007|Q2510|qid=Q2510|1=April 1}}.. Large rocks can be found scattered throughout this {{ill|lt=forest|Q4421|qid=Q4421|lt=forest|1=Forest}}, and these were worshipped as {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} (sacred rocks). === History === Since its founding in 736, the shrine was listed as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} and attracted great reverence, receiving the protection of the {{ill|lt=lords|Q1505781|qid=Q1505781|1=Castellan}} of {{ill|lt=Kakegawa Castle|Q2969457|qid=Q2969457|1=Kakegawa Castle}}. However, after the shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, maintenance of the shrine stagnated until the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. In the Meiji period, the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and restoration progressed, including the reconstruction of the {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} in {{ill|lt=1884|Q7819|qid=Q7819|1=1884}}. After another period of stagnation due to the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|qid=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}, further development was carried out, including the relocation of the main hall to the mountaintop in {{ill|lt=1987|Q2429|qid=Q2429|1=1987}}. === The Bell of Mugen on Mount Awagatake === This bell is counted as one of the {{ill|lt=Seven Mysteries of Enshū|Q11642075|qid=Q11642075|1=Seven Mysteries of Enshū}}. It was a {{ill|lt=Buddhist bell|Q8192099|qid=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} formerly located at Mugen-yama Kannon-ji, a neighboring temple. It was said that those who struck the bell would fall into {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Avīci Hell|1=UNKNOWN}} after death, but would gain immense wealth in this life. It is said that around the {{ill|lt=Meiō era|Q1193841|qid=Q1193841|1=Meiō}}, a concerned {{ill|lt=chief priest|Q1135621|qid=Q1135621|1=Abbot (Buddhism)}} sank the bell into a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|qid=Q43483|1=Well}}. This well remains within the precincts of Awawa Shrine“[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1347621/1/101 Tōkai Literature Walking Tour: Suruga and Tōtōmi Edition]” National Diet Library Digital Collection.. In later times, it came to be believed that throwing a broken branch of sakaki upside down into this well would confer the same merit as striking the bell. Kannon-ji was merged into Jōgen-ji Temple in Kakegawa City in {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|qid=Q3696|1=1961}} and subsequently abolished“[http://33kannon.com/temple-no27/%E6%B0%97%E3%81%BE%E3%81%90%E3%82%8C%E3%81%AA%E5%B7%A1%E7%A4%BC%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85%E3%89%91/ Capricious Pilgrimage Guide (21)]” Tōtōmi Thirty-three Kannon Sacred Sites.. === Notes === {{Footnote help}}
=== See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} * {{ill|lt=Gion faith|Q11588425|qid=Q11588425|1=Gion Faith}} * {{ill|lt=Gion Shrine|Q11588442|qid=Q11588442|1=Gion shrines}} * {{ill|lt=Chinju no Mori|Q11651158|qid=Q11651158|1=Chinju no Mori}} === External links === * [http://www10.plala.or.jp/awawajinjya/ Awawa Shrine Top Page] – Official {{ill|lt=website|Q35127|qid=Q35127|1=Website}}. {{Seven Mysteries of Enshū}} {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Awawa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Awawa Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Gion shrines]]
